_comment: Copy this file to config.json and fill in your values. config.json is gitignored. No personal data belongs in config.example.json.

operator.name: Your Name
operator.work_authorization: Authorized to work for any employer

search.target_role_type_notes: Define your own role-type taxonomy, e.g. R1=Product Leadership, R2=Governance, etc.

_criteria_comment: THE ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (fit/role_fit.py). These are YOUR values - the defaults
below are placeholders, not advice. ONE NUMERIC SYSTEM: every score in this
system is on [0.00, 1.00]. It is asserted in code, not hoped for.
HARD GATES run first and are unrescuable by score. Then the score. Then RANK and
cut to your weekly budget. Clearing the floor is permission to be RANKED, not
Why apply_floor is 0.60 and not 0.80: a job description is a WISH LIST. Nobody
meets 100% of one. Scoring against a wish list and demanding 0.80 rejects roles you
would win. Let the GATES disqualify; let the SCORE only rank what survives.

criteria.comp_floor_note: 0 disables the gate. An unstated salary is always a FLAG, never a reject - most boards omit it, and absence of evidence is not evidence.
criteria.level_words_note: Empty list disables the level gate.

keywords: your top target, e.g. ai governance
the career you are LEAVING - it matches, it should not excite

intersection.note: YOUR THESIS: the rare combination only you can claim. Scores 1.00 when BOTH sides are present, and much less when only one is.

mission_signals.note: Does the WORK align. Each signal is worth an equal share. This is NOT the company screen - see values_veto below.

example_sector: keyword that appears in such a JD

_values_veto_comment: THE COMPANY SCREEN (fit/values_veto.py). A GATE, not a scoring component.
You can match 99% of a job description and still not want to work there. A score
component can only DISCOUNT; a gate makes the role INVISIBLE. That is the difference,
and it is why this is not just another weight.
THREE STATES, and the agent NEVER breaks the tie:
FAIL    (<  fail_at)  -> suppressed, never shown again, but LOGGED for analytics
UNKNOWN (in between, or too little researched) -> ESCALATED TO YOU. Never guessed.
TWO DISCIPLINE RULES, ENFORCED IN CODE - they cannot be forgotten:
1. SILENCE IS NOT A PASS. An unresearched signal is null, NOT 'good'. More than
max_nulls unknowns forces UNKNOWN. A company with no public record would
otherwise sail through at a fake 1.00. Absence of evidence is not evidence.
2. NO SOURCE, NO CLAIM. An adverse finding about a NAMED INDIVIDUAL requires a
citable URL, or the code RAISES. If you suspect but cannot cite, return null -
that yields UNKNOWN, and a human rules. The system may NEVER manufacture a
FAIL by inference. This concerns real people.

values_veto.person_signals_note: Signals about NAMED HUMAN BEINGS. An adverse finding here REQUIRES a citable URL or Signal() raises. This is the anti-defamation guard.

hard_fails.discrimination_judgment: confirmed discrimination judgment or enforcement action
hard_fails.exploitation: exploitation of vulnerable or marginalized people

excluded_companies.reason: why you will not work here

values_veto.excluded_companies_note: A BLOCKLIST, NOT A VERDICT. Exclusion means exclusion. evaluate() and record() REFUSE on an excluded company, so no amount of research can silently overturn one. Aliases catch subsidiaries; corporate suffixes and TLDs are normalised away, so 'ExampleCorp.com, Inc.' still matches.
